one_time_description: NTC - BC Treaty Commission loan forgiveness as part of 2019 Federal Budget announcement forgiving all outstanding Treaty loans
economic_notes: Nation holds investments in multiple GBEs including Ehattesaht Fishing Corporation, Hayu Fishing LP, Aat'uu Forestry LP, Zeballos Lake Hydro LP, and others. Holds six foreshore licences for an oyster project. BC Gaming First Nations Revenue Sharing LP investment held at cost.
notes: Audit is qualified due to unaudited affiliated entities — auditors could not determine if adjustments were required for Investments in First Nation Affiliated Entities or Income from GBEs. Fiscal year is 2020, not 2024. Nation is in BC. NTC loan forgiveness of $1,143,669 is a significant one-time item. Eight GBEs accounted for by modified equity method; none fully consolidated. Ehattesaht Fishing Corporation reclassified from consolidated to GBE treatment starting March 31, 2019, causing a $624,453 adjustment to opening accumulated surplus. Wages and Benefits per Schedule of Expenses by Object is $761,396 but Segment Disclosure shows $763,357 — minor rounding difference; $761,396 used from Note 21. ISC funding not separately identified; Nuu-chah-nulth Tribal Council ($1,891,945) is the primary government transfer source, along with CMHC ($8,744) and Province of BC ($140,062) and First Peoples Heritage ($162,647) totalling $2,203,298 in identifiable government transfers. OSR includes GBE income, rent, road use, royalties, interest, and other.
